data.title: Blend Labs stock surges 33% after Q2 results beat, stock buyback announced
data.description:
Blend Labs (NYSE:BLND) stock soared 33% in early afternoon trading on Friday after the digital platform used by banks announced its first stock buyback, issued strong Q3 guidance, and delivered better-than-expected Q2 results.

The company adopted a $25M share buyback program, "which reflects our board’s confidence in our business and path ahead," Nima Ghamsari, head of Blend (BLND) said.

The company expects Q3 consolidated revenue of $39.5M-$43.5M (midpoint $41.5M vs. Visible Alpha consensus of $42.0M) and a non-GAAP net operating earnings of -$4.0M to -$7.0M (midpoint -$5.5M vs. Visible Alpha consensus of -$13.9M).

Q2 non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02, better than the -$0.04 consensus, narrowed from -$0.09 in Q2 2023.

Q2 total revenue of $40.5M, topping the $39.2M consensus, declined from $42.8M in the year-ago quarter.

Total non-GAAP operating expenses, though, dropped to $27.4M from $41.6M in last year's Q2.

During the quarter, Blend Labs (BLND) added several new mortgage customers, including Horizon Bank and First National Bank of Fort Smith.

It customer banking suite generated $8M of revenue in Q2, representing 37% Y/Y growth.

Mortgage suite revenue of $18.5M declined by 17% Y/Y.

Title segment revenue of $11.8M fell 5.6% Y/Y.

data.title: Kao GAAP basic EPS of ¥93.41, revenue of ¥788B
data.description:
• Kao press release (OTCPK:KAOCF): 1H GAAP basic EPS of ¥93.41.

data.title: AGCO shareholder pushes tractor maker for changes to board (update)
data.description:
Update 12:55 on Aug. 9: Adds AGCO response.

AGCO (NYSE:AGCO) is facing a request from its biggest shareholder for a shakeup of its board of directors, the maker of farm machinery said in a regulatory filing.

Tractors and Farm Equipment Ltd., which is based in India, seeks to replace AGCO (AGCO) directors outside of the yearly cycle of its shareholder meeting through a consent solicitation process. TAFE claimed that AGCO (AGCO) has wasted billions of dollars on bad deals, engaged in poor governance practices and has been outperformed by rivals such as Deere (DE).

TAFE held a 16% interest in AGCO (AGCO) as of April, Bloomberg News reported, citing data it had compiled.

AGCO responded on Wednesday that it maintains an open dialogue with all its investors and "welcomes constructive input toward the shared goal of maximizing stockholder value." AGCO pointed out that it filed notice of termination of certain commercial agreements with TAFE in April 2024.

"It is disappointing that it appears that TAFE has now chosen to respond to a commercial decision by threatening the use of the consent solicitation process in an attempt to pressure AGCO," AGCO said in the statement.

AGCO's (AGCO) machinery brands include Fendt and Massey Ferguson. The company notified TAFE in April that it was ending their commercial relationship.

data.title: Swire Pacific reports 1H results
data.description:
• Swire Pacific press release (OTCPK:SWRAY): 1H Recurring underlying profit of HK$4.8Bn.

data.title: Sleep Country Canada Holdings reports Q2 results

data.title: Lancashire Holdings GAAP EPS of $0.82
data.description:
• Lancashire Holdings press release (OTCPK:LCSHF): 1H GAAP EPS of $0.82.
  • Profit after tax of $200.8 million, resulting in a change in DBVS of 14.0%.
  • Gross premiums written increased 8.3% year-on-year to $1,282.2 million. Insurance revenue increased 18.5% year-on-year to $854.1 million.
  • Insurance service result of $222.8 million, discounted combined ratio of 73.0%, undiscounted combined ratio of 82.2%.
  • Total investment return of 2.3%, including unrealized gains and losses.
  • Interim dividend of 7.5 cents per common share.

data.title: New Fortress Energy sinks to nearly four-year low after earnings, sales miss
data.description:
New Fortress Energy (NASDAQ:NFE) -23.3% to a nearly four-year low in Friday's trading after reporting a larger than expected Q2 adjusted loss, following delays in launching a floating liquefied natural gas export facility off the Mexican coast.

The company swung to a Q2 net loss of $86.9M, or a loss of $0.44/share, from earnings of $120.1M, or $0.58/share, in the year-earlier quarter, while revenues slumped 24% Y/Y to $428M, also falling well short of analyst estimates; adjusted EBITDA fell by more than half from a year ago to $120.2M.

"The misses were entirely a result of the delay in the deployment of our first FLNG... asset," Chairman Wesley Edens said on the company's earnings conference call, according to Reuters.

New Fortress (NFE) has been trying to develop the floating LNG operation off the Mexican coast at Altamira, and Edens said it exported its first partial shipment of LNG on Friday morning.

"We performed our first transfer of a partial cargo earlier this morning," Edens said, adding the unit will shut down for a week for planned maintenance.

New Fortress (NFE) has forecast earnings of $275M per quarter for the rest of this year, but Edens warned on the call that Q3 results will be hurt by the late startup of the Altamira facility, which is not expected to achieve full production until September 1.

data.title: Energy Transfer announces pricing of units offering by certain holders
data.description:
Energy Transfer LP (NYSE:ET) has announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of over 38.75M of its common units representing limited partner interests by certain sellers.

The units are being offered at a price to the public of $15.78 each by WTG Midstream and an affiliate of Stonepeak.

The selling unitholders will receive all proceeds from the proposed offering. Energy Transfer (ET) will not sell any common units in the offering and will not receive any proceeds therefrom.

Certain officers and directors of the partnership’s general partner, LE GP, LLC have agreed to purchase 3.04M common units at the price per common unit paid by the underwriter to the selling unitholders. The underwriter will not receive any underwriting discounts or commissions under this offering for sales of common units to the affiliated purchasers.

Barclays is serving as the underwriter for the offering.

Shares of Energy Transfer LP (ET) were down 2.26% on Friday.

data.title: PowerFleet reports preliminary Q2 results
data.description:
• PowerFleet press release (NASDAQ:AIOT): Q2 Powerfleet anticipates reporting preliminary revenue for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 of approximately $75 million, representing an increase of 10% compared to the combined revenue of Powerfleet and MiX Telematics Ltd. in the same period last year.
  • Additionally, preliminary pro forma adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP metric, is projected to exceed $13.5 million, exclusive of $14 million in one-time expenses, reflecting an increase of over 40% versus the combined adjusted EBITDA from the prior year's comparable period.
  • Powerfleet is increasing its full-year fiscal 2025 guidance, forecasting revenue now to exceed $300 million versus prior guidance of approximately $300 million and adjusted EBITDA to exceed $60 million, which includes an incremental $5 million in secured exit run-rate cost synergies, versus the prior guidance of approximately $60 million.

data.title: Sweetgreen sees green on upbeat outlook
data.description:
Shares of Sweetgreen (NYSE:SG) are soaring in Friday’s trade after the fast-casual restaurant chain cut its Q2 loss by nearly half, saw same-store sales jump 9%, and raised its FY24 guidance for a second time this year, launching shares as much as 32% higher.

The introduction of steak dishes seems to have connected with customers as Q2 revenues increased 21% to $184.6M, driving down the company’s loss to just $0.13 per share compared to a loss of $0.24 in the same quarter last year. Meanwhile, same store sales were up 9% year-over-year, coinciding with the introduction of meat to the predominantly all-salad menu, compared to a more modest increase of 3% in the same quarter last year. The solid results, and the addition of new restaurants led the company to increase its FY24 guidance for a second time above Wall Street’s expectations. Guidance that UBS believes could be conservative.

“Second quarter results highlighted strong [same store sales] and traffic, solid margin expansion, and encouraging progress on Infinite Kitchen plans which support a compelling long-term growth outlook,” UBS analyst Dennis Geiger said. He maintained his Buy rating on Sweetgreen and lifted the target price by 19% to $37, a 40% premium to Thursday’s closing price.

Morgan Stanley remains neutral on Sweetgreen (SG) with an Equal Weight rating, but gave the price target a little boost to $27 as the firm was more cautious in its outlook given slowing consumer trends and outlook for the total addressable market, or TAM.

“There are still longer-term debates on TAM to be had and [how] this affects valuation, but the narrative is building on how both automation and menu broadening can expand the opportunity for [Sweetgreen],” Morgan Stanley’s Brian Harbour says.

But caution is warranted, CFO Mitch Reback said on the company’s earnings call. “We feel like we do not control the outside world, and we kind of read the same stuff in the same papers everybody else reads and reports on, and I think we have a degree of cautiousness around the external environment,” Reback said.

Morgan Stanley cites the challenging consumer environment along with any lingering cost inflation as a potential downside to the stock’s rating and price target, but success in automation and increasing margins should insulate the company from sustained shocks from changes in consumer spending.
